findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "request_oauth_integration_url" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

labels.name: list_dashboards
labels.description: List dashboards in your clariBI organization. Returns id, name, status, last refresh, and a URL you can open in a browser.

labels.name: list_data_sources
labels.description: List the data sources connected to your clariBI organization. Returns id, name, source_type, status, last sync time, and the number of rows (when known).

labels.name: list_forecasts
labels.description: List metric forecasts in your clariBI organization. Each row covers one metric: its source binding, horizon, schedule, and the latest run status. Use get_forecast_run to fetch the full forecast result.

labels.name: list_reports
labels.description: List generated reports in your clariBI organization. Returns id, title, status, output format, and download URL.

labels.name: regenerate_forecast_narrative
labels.description: Re-run ONLY the AI narration step against an existing completed forecast run. Costs 1-2 AI credits (no engine work). Returns the new narration; the old one is overwritten in the run record. Refuses when the run is not yet complete or when narration_enabled=false on the forecast.

labels.name: register_account
labels.description: Begin clariBI account signup. Validates the email + organization name, emails a 6-digit verification code, and returns a pending_id. Call verify_email(pending_id, code) within 10 minutes to finish signup and receive an API key.

labels.name: request_oauth_integration_url
labels.description: Initiate an OAuth handoff to a vendor integration (Google Ads, GA4, Search Console, Sheets, Drive, BigQuery, Meta Ads, Jira, Confluence). Returns an authorization URL the user opens in a browser. After the user clicks Allow, the connection is created and you can poll check_integration_status(handoff

labels.name: run_analysis
labels.description: Run a natural-language analytics question against your connected data sources. Consumes AI credits. Returns either the completed analysis result inline OR a job_id you can poll with get_analysis_status. If list_data_sources returns an empty list, ingest data first with upload_data_source (inline bas

labels.name: run_forecast
labels.description: Run a forecast now. Reserves AI credits up front, dispatches the backtest + projection + correlation pipeline, and returns either the completed result inline (wait_seconds > 0 and the run finishes in time) or a run_id you can poll with get_forecast_run.

labels.name: upload_data_source
labels.description: Create a new data source from an inline base64-encoded file (CSV, TSV, JSON, Excel, TXT, PDF). The file goes through the same validation and preprocessing as a web upload. Returns the data_source_id you can pass to run_analysis as soon as preprocessing completes (poll get_data_source_schema for read

labels.name: verify_email
labels.description: Complete clariBI signup by submitting the verification code plus a password. Returns an OAuth access_token for immediate use AND a long-lived MCP API key for persistent configuration. The new organization lands on the Trial tier (50 AI credits, 14 days).

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "v1" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_checkout_session, get_dashboard, list_dashboards, list_reports, register_account, request_oauth_integration_url, run_analysis · Private data: ingest_url_data_source, regenerate_forecast_narrative, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Public sink: ingest_url_data_source, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Destructive: create_forecast, get_forecast_run, get_forecast_trust, list_forecasts, list_reports, regenerate_forecast_narrative, run_analysis, run_forecast.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_checkout_session, get_dashboard, list_dashboards, list_reports, register_account, request_oauth_integration_url, run_analysis · Private data: ingest_url_data_source, regenerate_forecast_narrative, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Public sink: ingest_url_data_source, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Destructive: create_forecast, get_forecast_run, get_forecast_trust, list_forecasts, list_reports, regenerate_forecast_narrative, run_analysis, run_forecast

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
